description: A bare `@x = expr` write to a cell with no prior structural `<x>` declaration in scope fires E-STATE-UNDECLARED (§6.1.2). Inside `function f()`, `@undecl = 42` writes a cell that was never declared via `<undecl>`, so the post-CE type-system stage reports E-STATE-UNDECLARED (the auto-synth path that once silently promoted such writes is removed). Incidental W-PROGRAM-SPA-INFERRED is ignored by superset matching.
rationale: §6.1.2 state cells are declared structurally (`<x> = init`); a bare `@x = expr` write with no such declaration in scope is a compile error (E-STATE-UNDECLARED), never a silent auto-declaration.
